dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3553 IS: Where’s the Workforce At Listed by Duties and Office
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Ernst introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs

form.official-title: To require the Director of the Office of Personnel Management to establish a searchable, publicly available website that contains information regarding the Federal civilian workforce, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Where’s the Workforce At Listed by Duties and Office or the Where’s WALDO Act.

paragraph.text: The term civil service has the meaning given the term in section 2101 of title 5, United States Code.

subparagraph.text: an employee of a contractor; or
in the case of a contractor who is an individual who directly enters into a contract with the Federal Government, that individual.

paragraph.text: The term contractor has the meaning given the term in section 7101 of title 41, United States Code.
The term Director means the Director of the Office of Personnel Management.

subsection.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this Act, the Director shall establish, and thereafter the Director shall maintain, a searchable, publicly available website that contains information regarding each individual occupying a position in the civil service, which shall include, with respect to each such individual—

paragraph.text: the title of the position occupied by the individual;
a description of the duties of the position occupied by the individual;
the employing agency of the individual;
the primary duty station of the individual;
the annual rate of basic pay of the individual, including, with respect to a year, any bonus or supplemental wages paid to the individual; and
the date on which the individual was appointed to the position occupied by the individual.
Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act, and annually thereafter, the Director shall make publicly available a report that details, for the year covered by the report—

subparagraph.text: the total number of contract employees that worked, on a part-time or full-time basis, under applicable contracts with the Federal Government; and
the total cost incurred by the Federal Government with respect to the work described in subparagraph (A).

paragraph.text: The data contained in each report made available under paragraph (1) shall be disaggregated by agency.
